message:
fix(engine): continued numeric safety — saturating/wrapping arithmetic for all counters and sequence numbers

Systematic pass replacing bare += 1 increments on u64 counters with
saturating_add(1) to prevent silent wraparound at u64::MAX. While
overflow is astronomically unlikely in practice, saturating_add makes
the intent explicit and matches the pattern established in the prior
hardening commit.

Files affected (all counter/sequence ID increments):
- causal_replay: NondeterminismLog::next_sequence
- deterministic_sim_scheduler: SimScheduler::next_event_id
- epoch_invalidation: EpochInvalidationEngine::event_seq
- evidence_emission: CanonicalEvidenceEmitter::next_sequence
- execution_orchestrator: ExecutionOrchestrator::execution_counter
- hostcall_telemetry: TelemetryRecorder::next_record_id
- runtime_decision_core: decision_count, calibration_seq, audit_trail seq
- runtime_decision_theory: DecisionContext::sequence
- scheduler_lane: LaneScheduler::next_task_id
- translation_validation_receipt: ReceiptChain::next_sequence
- wasm_runtime_lane: WasmSignalGraph::next_id
- workload_policy_manifold: ManifoldPlacer::next_sequence

Special case — seqlock_fastpath: uses wrapping_add(1) and wrapping_add(2)
for atomic sequence numbers, where wrapping is the correct semantic (seqlock
readers detect torn writes via odd/even parity, so wrapping preserves the
protocol invariant).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
